relations.reasoning: The data explicitly states they are located a few meters apart.
Both are located in the municipality of Giurdignano and are geographically proximate.
Both are identified as typical Salento dolmens with less articulated plans compared to central Puglia.
Both are dated to the Early Bronze Age.

paths.title: The Salento Megalithic Trail
paths.narrative: A journey through the heart of Salento to explore the evolution of Bronze Age funerary monuments, focusing on the transition from simple single-cell structures to more complex arrangements.

paths.title: Sardinian Ancient Landscapes
paths.narrative: An exploration of the oldest megalithic structures in Sardinia, dating back to the V-III millennium BC, highlighting the deep roots of the island's prehistoric culture.

raw_response.overall_observations: The collection reveals a clear dichotomy between the Bronze Age megalithic traditions of the Salento peninsula (Puglia), characterized by single-cell funerary structures, and the older V-III millennium BC megalithic tradition of Sardinia. The Salento sites show a high degree of typological homogeneity, while the Sardinian sites are more dispersed across various municipalities, reflecting a widespread and long-standing megalithic culture.
